verdict_reason: The disagreement entry on 'kāmātmānaḥ ... bhoga-aiśvarya-gatim prati' has substance. It correctly identifies the constitutive force of kāmātman (not 'people who happen to have desires' but 'people whose ātman/very nature is constituted as desire') — Shankara's emphasis on identity-structured-by-desire and Ramanuja's 'attached to secular desires that block other goals from registering' are both quoted and preserved without conflation. The explanation correctly notes that modern translations (Prabhupada, Mukundananda) flatten kāmātman to 'desirous' and lose the constitutive force. The explanation also correctly states that bhoga-aiśvarya-gati is named as a real trajectory, not denied — the verse is not 'rituals fail' but 'these are real attainments at this level, and this level is bounded.' Substantive. PASS.
